# @verevoir/accelerator

The aigency accelerator — the commodity developer toolbelt as an MCP server, plus
the substrate/LLM/telemetry/loop primitives it exposes as a library. Split out of
@verevoir/mcp (STDIO-523): this package is everything that is NOT the moat. The
governed capabilities live in the private @verevoir/capabilities, which composes
on the substrate exported here (dependency direction: capabilities -> accelerator).

accelerator makes NO LLM calls. It only reads/writes sources and work trackers, so
it only ever needs source/board credentials — never a model API key.

## Install

Public, two ways:

    npx -y @verevoir/accelerator                      # npm (published)
    npx -y github:verevoir/accelerator#v0.1.5         # from the repo (builds on install)

MCP client config: { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@verevoir/accelerator"] }

## Secrets / environment — and why

Only source/board access. No model keys belong here (accelerator makes no model
calls; putting one here is dead config + needless exposure). This asymmetry is the
split: commodity server gets the code/board keys, the moat gets the model keys.

- GITHUB_TOKEN — GitHub source reads + writes/PRs (read_file, grep, find_symbol,
  code_graph, write_file, edit_file, multi_edit, insert, delete_block, commit_files,
  ensure_fork, ensure_branch, open_pull_request).
- NOTION_API_KEY — Notion source + the Notion work-tracker board.
- TRELLO_API_KEY / TRELLO_API_TOKEN / TRELLO_REFERER — Trello work-tracker.
- AIGENCY_AUDIT / AIGENCY_AUDIT_DIR — emit audit spans + where to write them.
- OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT — OTLP export of spans.

Public repos / local paths need no token; tokens gate private sources + writes.

## Tools (22)

Source (cached, tree-sitter indexed): read_file, list_files, get_repo_tree, grep,
find_symbol, code_graph, write_file, edit_file, multi_edit, insert, delete_block,
commit_files, ensure_fork, ensure_branch, open_pull_request. Work tracker: list_cards, get_card, create_card, update_card,
move_card, list_columns, list_comments, add_comment. Prefer these over built-in
filesystem/shell so the shared cache + index stay correct.

grep is lazy (via @verevoir/context 0.14): it reads the source in deterministic
order and stops once maxResults matches are settled, warming only the files it
reads — a small query no longer touches every file; the hits are identical to a
whole-source warm + search. An optional prefix scopes grep/warm to one subtree.

## Library (subpath exports)

Every compiled module is importable by subpath: @verevoir/accelerator/tiers,
/router, /audit, /metering, /result, /edit, /cache, /mutate, /graph, /architecture, /manifest,
/instructions, /loop/evals, /loop/refine, /loop/search, /tools/source,
/tools/workflow. @verevoir/capabilities imports these.

## Antagonistic-review gate

This repo runs the gate; the reviewer's provision comes from the private
@verevoir/capabilities (git-installed per .github/antagonistic-review/mcp.json).
Credentials: a GitHub App (org secrets VEREVOIR_APP_ID + VEREVOIR_APP_PRIVATE_KEY)
mints a per-run, least-privilege token scoped to just the repos the gate reads —
no standing PAT — plus a reviewer model credential (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, or
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Gate scripts: aggregate.sh (decision) + resolve-merge-base.sh
(diff range). Full gate config is in the capabilities docs (it is the reviewer
engine).

## Bins

accelerator / verevoir-accelerator (server), verevoir-card-sync, verevoir-audit-trace.

## Publishing / consumption

Public on npm; also consumable via git-install (github:verevoir/accelerator#vX.Y.Z,
built on install by its prepare script). @verevoir/capabilities depends on it via
the git form, so the moat needs no registry.
